Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The objective is to analyze a provided event log of a Customer Support Ticket Resolution process I need to identify cases with long resolution times determine the root causes bottlenecks delays escalations and provide recommendations to improve performance

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Analyze the Data I need to process the provided table row by row calculating durations for each case and identifying the flow of activities

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 101 Start 2024 03 01 08 00 End 2024 03 01 10 15 Total Duration 2 hours 15 minutes Flow Receive Triage 10m Assign 10m Investigate 40m Resolve 60m Close 15m Observation Very fast no escalation

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 102 Start 2024 03 01 08 05 End 2024 03 02 09 15 Total Duration 25 hours 10 minutes

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait check timestamps 09 00 Assign 11 30 Escalate 14 00 Investigate 09 00 Resolve Next Day

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Gap from Assign 2 5 hours Gap from Escalate 2 5 hours Gap from Investigate 19 hours Observation Significant delay between Investigation and Resolution Escalation occurred

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 103 Start 2024 03 01 08 10 End 2024 03 01 09 30 Total Duration 1 hour 20 minutes Flow Receive Triage 5m Assign 15m Investigate 15m Resolve 30m Close 15m Observation Very fast no escalation

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 104 Start 2024 03 01 08 20 End 2024 03 02 08 30 Total Duration 24 hours 10 minutes

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait check timestamps 09 30 Assign 13 00 Investigate Gap 3 5 hours 08 00 Resolve Next Day Gap 19 hours

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Observation No escalation but huge gap between Investigate and Resolve Triage also took longer than 101 and 103

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 105 Start 2024 03 01 08 25 End 2024 03 03 09 30 Total Duration 49 hours

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait check timestamps 03 01 10 00 Escalate 03 02 14 00 Investigate Gap 28 hours 03 03 09 00 Resolve Gap 19 hours

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Observation Longest case Multiple delays Escalation involved

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Fast 101 2h 15m 103 1h 20m Slow 102 25h 104 24h 105 49h Outlier 105 is the worst

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Root Causes Escalations Cases 102 and 105 have escalations Case 104 does not but is still slow

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Waiting Times 102 19 hour gap between Investigation and Resolution 104 19 hour gap between Investigation and Resolution and 3 5 hour wait before Investigation 105 28 hour gap between Escalation and Investigation and 19 hour gap between Investigation and Resolution

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Weekend or Off hours The logs span overnight This suggests work is happening during business hours and tickets sit overnight

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: It implies Level 1 agents might be bottlenecks even without escalation or the Investigate step itself is stalling

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Did I calculate Case 105 correctly Total 49 hours Correct Did I calculate Case 104 correctly Total 24 hours 10 mins Correct Did I calculate Case 102 correctly Total 25 hours 10 mins Correct

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Is Case 104 delay due to escalation No there is no Escalate row for 104 This is a crucial distinction

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Pattern The Overnight Gap approximately 19 to 28 hours is the primary driver Secondary Pattern Escalations add complexity and handover time Tertiary Pattern Triage and Assignment efficiency varies

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Recommendations Reduce overnight waits improve Level 1 capability to reduce escalations or improve Level 2 capacity monitor the Investigate to Resolve gap specifically standardize Triage times
